# Public Finance — Regulatory Frameworks

## International Public-Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS)

| Standard  | Title                                       | Code Implication                                |
|-----------|---------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| IPSAS 1   | Presentation of Financial Statements        | Mandatory comparatives; going-concern disclosure|
| IPSAS 2   | Cash Flow Statements                        | Direct method preferred; three activity classes |
| IPSAS 12  | Inventories                                 | Government stores at lower of cost / NRV        |
| IPSAS 17  | Property, Plant and Equipment               | Cost model or revaluation; depreciation required|
| IPSAS 19  | Provisions, Contingent Liabilities & Assets | Disclosure rules; probability > 50%             |
| IPSAS 20  | Related Party Disclosures                   | Ministers, senior officials, spouses disclosed  |
| IPSAS 24  | Presentation of Budget Information          | Approved budget vs. actuals; material variances |
| IPSAS 32  | Service Concession Arrangements (PPP)       | Asset recognition; liability on balance sheet   |
| IPSAS 40  | Public Sector Combinations                  | Acquisition vs. amalgamation accounting         |
| IPSAS 41  | Financial Instruments                       | Derivatives, bonds: fair value / amortised cost |

## European System of Accounts (ESA 2010)

| Concept                | Definition                                              |
|------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| General Government Sector | S.13 — central, state, local government + social security |
| Government Deficit (EDP) | Net borrowing in national accounts terms               |
| Maastricht Debt        | Consolidated gross debt at face value                   |
| COFOG                  | Classification of Functions of Government (10 divisions)|
| Accrual Basis          | All flows recorded at time of economic activity         |

## Government Finance Statistics (GFS — IMF)

| Component               | Description                                              |
|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| Revenue                 | Taxes, social contributions, grants, other revenue       |
| Expenditure             | Compensation, use of goods/services, interest, transfers |
| Net Operating Balance   | Revenue − Expenditure (excl. transactions in NFA)        |
| Net Lending/Borrowing   | Final GFS deficit/surplus measure                        |
| Financial Assets        | Currency, deposits, securities, loans                    |
| Liabilities             | Debt securities, loans, other liabilities                |

## Public Procurement Framework (EU Directive 2014/24)

| Threshold (2024–2025)   | Procedure                     |
|-------------------------|-------------------------------|
| < €140,000 (goods/services) | National simplified rules |
| ≥ €140,000 (central govt) | EU Journal publication required|
| ≥ €5,382,000 (works)    | Full OJEU procedure           |
| All above threshold     | 80-day response minimum       |

## UK Public Finance Legislation

| Act / Regulation                       | Key Provision                               |
|----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Government Resources and Accounts Act 2000 | Consolidated Fund; supply estimates    |
| Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 | OBR independent forecasts      |
| Public Contracts Regulations 2015      | UK procurement rules post-Brexit            |
| Finance Acts (annual)                  | Tax and duty rates; Treasury directions     |
